wiki.summary: “Weird Fishes / Arpeggi” brandishes new drums behind its drain-circling arpeggios, but sounds every bit as massive in crescendoing as its live renditions suggested it might. In an interview with The Quietus, Jonny Greenwood was asked: "Has your work as a composer changed your approach with Radiohead? I was at the Ether Festival when you performed ‘Arpeggi’ as an orchestral arrangement, long before the release of In Rainbows – where ‘Arpeggi’ was recorded with a conventional band arrangement." Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: “Weird Fishes / Arpeggi” brandishes new drums behind its drain-circling arpeggios, but sounds every bit as massive in crescendoing as its live renditions suggested it might. In an interview with The Quietus, Jonny Greenwood was asked: "Has your work as a composer changed your approach with Radiohead? I was at the Ether Festival when you performed ‘Arpeggi’ as an orchestral arrangement, long before the release of In Rainbows – where ‘Arpeggi’ was recorded with a conventional band arrangement." And Greenwood replied: "I’m still keen to approach a Radiohead song by going to orchestra first instead of orchestra last, as is traditional." Read more on Last.fm.
